vitals.terrain: Hilly with steep climbs and descents
vitals.participation_trend: Stable regional event attracting elite and recreational riders

course_description.overview: Epic circuit traversing the entire Coromandel Peninsula through subtropical forests, Pacific coastlines, rural farmland, and Pohutukawa coastline of the Hauraki Gulf. Features 2300m of screaming descent and is considered one of the toughest one-day challenges in the Southern Hemisphere.

key_segments.name: Major Climb
key_segments.description: Steep ascents contributing to 2300m total elevation, named after challenging K2 mountain coincidence.

key_segments.name: Coastal Sections
key_segments.description: Pacific coastline and Hauraki Gulf with scenic Pohutukawa views.

key_segments.name: Full Peninsula Loop
key_segments.description: Loop starting from varying towns like Thames or Coromandel, covering diverse terrain.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Possibly the toughest one-day cycle challenge in the Southern Hemisphere due to length, elevation, and climbs; attracts elite riders yet open to weekend warriors and recreational cyclists.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Auckland Airport (AKL), then drive 2-3 hours to Coromandel Peninsula towns like Thames or Coromandel. Starts from different towns annually.
logistics.accommodation: Available in Coromandel town, Thames, and coastal communities like Kuaotunu; book early for event weekends.
logistics.registration_details: Organized by ARC Events (Adventure Racing Coromandel) or CycleEvents; check cycleevents.com or gostride.co.nz for entries.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bike required; transport via car racks or shuttles from Auckland common.
logistics.local_considerations: Subtropical climate with potential rain; watch for coastal winds and traffic on public roads.

final_verdict.one_liner: Toughest one-day road challenge on New Zealand's stunning Coromandel Peninsula.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Strong road cyclists seeking epic distance, climbs, and scenery; from elites to ambitious amateurs.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the notable combination of challenge, beauty, and regional prestige.
pro_tips: Train for 2000m+ climbs and long descents; practice steep NZ roads.
Start early to beat heat and winds; fuel for 8-12 hour efforts.
Check start town annually; stay in Coromandel for vibes.
Bring rain gear for subtropical weather shifts.
